function.name: omicsclaw
function.description:
Run an OmicsClaw multi-omics analysis skill.

(test)

Defaults: `skill='auto'` + `query=<user request>` (auto-routes deterministically). Pick a specific skill only if the user named it. `mode='demo'` ONLY when explicitly asked. `return_media` empty = text summary; pass a keyword ('umap','qc','cluster',comma-sep) or 'all' for figures. For sc-batch-integration upstream-prep pause, rerun with `auto_prepare=true`. Preserve exact numbers / paths / errors.

skill.description: Skill alias to run. Default to 'auto' and pass `query`; use a specific alias only if the user named it or auto-routing asked you to disambiguate.

mode.description: 'demo' = built-in synthetic data; 'file' = user uploaded a file via messaging; 'path' = user provided a file path on the server.

return_media.description: Filter for which figures/tables to send back. Omit or leave empty for text summary only (default). 'all' = send all figures and tables. Otherwise a comma-separated list of keywords to match filenames (e.g. 'umap', 'qc', 'violin', 'cluster', 'umap,qc'). Only set when the user explicitly asks for visual results.

file_path.description: Server-side file path or filename for mode='path'.

batch_key.description: Batch/sample metadata column passed as --batch-key. Especially important for sc-batch-integration. If omitted for sc-batch-integration, OmicsClaw will inspect the AnnData object and ask the user to choose before running.

confirm_workflow_skip.description: Only for explicit user overrides. If true, bypasses the standardize/preprocess workflow pause for sc-batch-integration and runs direct integration anyway.

auto_prepare.description: Only when the user explicitly agrees to preparatory steps. For sc-batch-integration, automatically runs the recommended upstream workflow (`sc-standardize-input` and/or `sc-preprocessing`) before the final integration step.

n_epochs.description: Number of training epochs for deep learning methods. Defaults per method if omitted: cell2location=30000, destvi=2500, stereoscope=150000, tangram=1000. Only set when the user explicitly requests a custom epoch count.

function.name: list_skills_in_domain
function.description: Lazy-load the full skill list for one OmicsClaw domain. Call this ONLY when the 8-domain briefing in the `omicsclaw` tool description isn't enough to pick a skill, or when the user asks 'what tools do you have for <domain>?'. The result is a markdown block with each skill's alias, one-line description, and trigger keywords. For most routing, prefer `omicsclaw(skill='auto', query=...)` — the resolver picks the right skill without this extra round-trip.

filter.description: Optional case-insensitive substring. Matches against skill alias, description, and trigger keywords. Omit to see the whole domain.

function.name: save_file
function.description: Save a file that was sent via messaging to a specific folder. Default: OmicsClaw data/ directory.

function.name: write_file
function.description: Create or overwrite a file with the given content. Files are saved to the output/ directory by default. ONLY use when user explicitly asks to create/save a file.

function.name: parse_literature
function.description: Parse scientific literature (PDF, URL, DOI, PubMed ID) to extract GEO accessions and metadata, then download datasets. Use when user mentions a paper, sends a PDF, or provides a literature reference.

input_value.description: URL, DOI, PubMed ID, file path, or text content

function.name: fetch_geo_metadata
function.description: Fetch metadata for a specific GEO accession (GSE, GSM, or GPL). Use when user asks to fetch, query, or get information about a specific GEO ID.

download.description: Download the dataset after fetching metadata (default: false)

function.name: list_directory
function.description: List contents of a directory. Use when user wants to see files in a folder.

function.name: inspect_file
function.description: Display contents of a CSV, JSON, or TXT file. Use when user wants to view file contents.

function.name: make_directory
function.description: Create a new directory under output/. ONLY use when user explicitly asks to create a folder.

function.name: move_file
function.description: Move or rename a file. ONLY use when user explicitly asks to move or rename files.

function.name: remove_file
function.description: Delete a file or directory. ONLY use when user explicitly asks to remove files/folders.

path.description: File or directory path to remove

function.name: get_file_size
function.description: Get file size in MB. Use when user asks about file size.

function.name: read_knowhow
function.description: Fetch the full markdown body of a Mandatory Scientific Constraint (KnowHow guard) by name. Call this when the headline-only Active Guards block in the system prompt does not give enough detail — for example, when the user asks WHY a guard requires a specific threshold, when a method name is ambiguous, or when the headline mentions parameters the user is uncertain about. Accepts the filename (e.g. KH-sc-de-guardrails.md), the doc_id (sc-de-guardrails), or the human-readable label. Returns the full markdown text, or an empty string if no guard matches.

name.description: KH identifier. Accepts filename (KH-sc-de-guardrails.md), doc_id (sc-de-guardrails), or label (Single-Cell Differential Expression Guardrails).

function.name: consult_knowledge
function.description: Query the OmicsClaw knowledge base for analysis guidance. Use this PROACTIVELY when: (1) user is unsure which analysis to run, (2) user asks about method selection or parameters, (3) an analysis fails and user needs troubleshooting, (4) user asks 'how to' or 'which method' questions, (5) before running complex analyses to check best practices. Returns relevant decision guides, best practices, or troubleshooting advice.

query.description: Natural language question about methodology, parameters, or troubleshooting

function.name: resolve_capability
function.description: Resolve whether a user request is fully covered by an existing OmicsClaw skill, partially covered, or not covered. Use this before non-trivial analysis requests when skill coverage is uncertain.

query.description: The user's analysis request in natural language.

file_path.description: Optional server-side data path or filename mentioned by the user.

domain_hint.description: Optional domain hint if the user has already narrowed the domain.

function.name: inspect_data
function.description: Instantly inspect an AnnData (.h5ad) file's metadata — cell/gene counts, obs/var columns, embeddings (obsm), layers, uns keys — WITHOUT loading the expression matrix. Fast even for very large files. Also supports pre-run method suitability and parameter preview when `method` (and optionally `skill`) is provided. ALWAYS call this when the user asks to 'explore', 'inspect', 'what can I do with', or vaguely 'analyze' a dataset WITHOUT specifying a concrete analysis pipeline. Returns a formatted summary and suggested analysis directions. Do NOT call omicsclaw for open-ended exploratory queries.

file_path.description: Path to the .h5ad file to inspect.

skill.description: Optional skill alias for preflight preview (e.g. spatial-domain-identification).

method.description: Optional method name for suitability + parameter preview.

preview_params.description: If true, include a pre-run method suitability and default parameter preview block.

function.name: tool_search
function.description: Search available OmicsClaw tools by name, description, and tags. Use this before assuming a tool exists.

query.description: Search text for tool names, capabilities, or tags.

limit.description: Maximum number of tools to return. Default: 12.

include_schema.description: Include parameter names in the response.

function.name: file_read
function.description: Read a text file from the active workspace, pipeline workspace, or trusted project directories.

end_line.description: 1-based ending line number. Default: end of file.

function.name: glob_files
function.description: Find files under the active workspace or project directories using a glob pattern.

root.description: Optional root directory to search from.

limit.description: Maximum number of matches to return. Default: 200.

function.name: grep_files
function.description: Search text files under the active workspace or project directories using a regular expression.

root.description: Optional root directory to search from.

glob.description: Optional glob filter for candidate files. Default: '**/*'.

max_matches.description: Maximum number of matches to return. Default: 50.

function.name: file_write
function.description: Create or overwrite a text file inside the active workspace or a safe engineering output workspace.

create_dirs.description: Create parent directories when missing. Default: true.

function.name: file_edit
function.description: Perform a controlled text replacement inside an existing text file.

replace_all.description: Replace all matches instead of exactly one match.
